task_completion.description: Verify that a new folder named 'Training-Examples' has been created in the email client.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the folder pane/sidebar in the email client. There should be a folder explicitly named 'Training-Examples'.

task_completion.description: Verify that at least five distinct phishing/spam emails have been moved into the 'Training-Examples' folder.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the contents of the 'Training-Examples' folder. The message list should contain at least 5 distinct emails that appear to be spam/phishing (e.g., suspicious subjects, senders).

task_completion.description: Verify that a new email composition window is open or a draft is saved.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An email composition window or a draft view is visible in the email client.

task_completion.description: Verify that the recipient of the new email is set to 'all-staff@company.com'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'To' field in the email composition window contains exactly 'all-staff@company.com'.

task_completion.description: Verify that the subject of the new email is 'Upcoming Phishing Awareness Training'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Subject' field in the email composition window contains exactly 'Upcoming Phishing Awareness Training'.

task_completion.description: Verify that the body of the email describes at least three specific phishing techniques.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The email body contains text explaining phishing techniques, using keywords from at least 3 distinct categories (e.g., deception/spoofing, urgency/prizes, commercial/discounts, financial/bank, technical/links).

integrity.description: Verify the agent used an actual email client GUI rather than terminal commands or text editors to manipulate emails.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show an email client interface (e.g., Thunderbird) being actively used to move emails and compose the message, not just terminal windows or text editors.

integrity.description: Verify the emails in the 'Training-Examples' folder are actual emails from the Junk folder, not newly created dummy emails.
integrity.visual_evidence: The emails in the target folder should have realistic headers, dates, and content typical of real spam, rather than simple test messages typed by the agent.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not use a Python script or terminal command to bypass the GUI and directly write to the maildir/mbox files.
integrity.visual_evidence: Absence of terminal windows running scripts to move files or generate the draft email behind the scenes.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target folder name: 'Training-Examples'. Minimum spam emails required in the folder: 5. Announcement email recipient: 'all-staff@company.com'. Announcement email subject: 'Upcoming Phishing Awareness Training'. The email body must contain keywords from at least 3 of the following 5 categories: 1) deception (e.g., phishing, spoof, fake), 2) urgency (e.g., urgent, prize, winner, immediate), 3) commercial (e.g., pharma, discount, cheap), 4) financial (e.g., money, bank, account, wire), 5) technical (e.g., link, click, attachment).
